The classification is based on its botanical characteristics and phylogenetic relationships with other related species. intro:
I couldn't find any information on the plant Eugenia geraensis. However, I can give you information about the Eugenia genus which includes many species. 

Eugenia is a genus of flowering plants in the family Myrtaceae, commonly known as the myrtle family. The Eugenia genus includes over 1,000 species of shrubs and small trees, many of which are native to tropical and subtropical regions of the world.

Some Eugenia species, such as Eugenia uniflora, have been found to have several health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Eugenia species include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils.

The most common herbal preparations made with Eugenia species include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, skin problems, and respiratory infections.
